warnings: MET emits violation observations only; it does not enforce, decide, or approve. CDE is the canonical decision authority and SEL is the canonical enforcement authority.
Violations are derived from the absence of governed-loop artifacts (AEX/PQX/EVL/TPA/CDE/SEL legs) for AI programming work items.
When upstream evidence is absent, this record does not infer that the leg is present; missing artifacts surface as missing legs.

violation_count updated from 4 to 6 in record v002: AIPG-CODEX-001 (AEX missing) and AIPG-CLAUDE-001 (PQX missing) are now represented as explicit violations backed by source evidence records in artifacts/ai_programming/.
failure_prevented: AI programming (Codex + Claude) repo mutations passing through the dashboard without an artifact-backed AEX → PQX → EVL → TPA → CDE → SEL trail being silently rendered as PASS.
signal_improved: Each AI programming work item that bypasses any required core-loop leg surfaces as a named violation with the missing leg, agent type, source evidence ref, and next recommended input. All six violations are now backed by source evidence in artifacts/ai_programming/.

violations.why_it_matters: AEX is the admission/intake leg. Without a governed AEX record for this Codex-driven repo mutation, downstream legs cannot be lineage-bound to a structured input. The existing aex_admission_evidence_record references req-aex-trust-01-1, not AIPG-CODEX-001.
violations.next_recommended_input: Produce an AEX intake record naming work_item_id=AIPG-CODEX-001 and link it as an upstream source for the PQX execution record. Human review required to confirm retroactive AEX evidence is permissible.

violations.why_it_matters: PQX is the sole execution authority. A repo-mutating change with no PQX execution record cannot be replayed and bypasses the only governed execution boundary.
